title: callicarpa coriacea
intro: Callicarpa coriacea, also known as the purple bell vine, is a plant species that belongs to the balsam family (Lamiaceae) and is native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Callicarpa coriacea include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant is typically prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
